title: chenopodium album subsp. borbasiiforme
intro: Chenopodium album subsp. borbasiiforme, also known as lamb's quarters or goosefoot, is a subspecies of the Chenopodium album plant, which is a type of flowering plant in the family Amaranthaceae. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Chenopodium album subsp. borbasiiforme include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, making it a valuable herb for overall well-being. Herbal preparations of Chenopodium album subsp. borbasiiforme can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it can also be consumed as a raw vegetable or used in cooking.
